response.justification: Exceptional technical depth in developer tools, CI/CD infrastructure, and testing frameworks—exactly the domain this role requires—but critically lacks the 2+ years of mandatory people management and team leadership experience. The resume shows a high-performing individual contributor and technical mentor, not a people manager. Recent promotion to Staff level (one month ago) is promising for technical growth but doesn't address the absence of formal people leadership experience essential for this people-leadership manager role.
strengths: 20+ years of software development with 10+ years building developer tools, CI/CD infrastructure, testing libraries, and deployment automation—precisely the technical focus area needed
Deep expertise across compilers, build systems, testing frameworks, and infrastructure automation demonstrated through professional roles and open-source projects like Fluent-Asserts and Trial
Strong teaching and mentoring ability evidenced through workshops, courses, community leadership, and organizing refactoring dojos—translatable communication skills for management
concerns: Zero years of formal people management experience; job explicitly requires minimum 2 years of team leadership, hiring, performance reviews, and 1-on-1s
Career trajectory is entirely individual-contributor and staff-engineer track with no evidence of managing reports, team performance, budgets, or organizational change
Only one month into current Staff level role; no demonstrable progression into people management or evidence of readiness to transition from IC to manager responsibilities

key_factors.factor: Expert-level technical knowledge in developer tools and infrastructure
